own_review.issue: long->int truncation: `int len = (int)(count() - transferred())` and `int offset = (int)(position() + transferred())` in spliceToPipe, plus the `int transferred` field, break FileRegions with count or position+transferred > 2GB (negative/wrong lengths and offsets, corrupt transfer). newSplice accepts long off_in, so the offset cast is gratuitous.

scheduleWriteSingle returns 0 when fileRegion.open() throws IOException, but AbstractIoUringChannel.scheduleWrite asserts numOutstandingWrites > 0 (AbstractIoUringChannel.java:332) -> AssertionError when assertions are enabled.

ECANCELED regression: the IoUringFileRegion branch calls ioResult("io_uring splice", res) before the existing `res == Native.ERRNO_ECANCELED_NEGATIVE -> return true` check that the ByteBuf path retains, so a cancelled splice (normal during close) is converted into an exception fed to handleWriteError.
Public constructor of public class IoUringIoOps changed signature (spliceFdIn inserted before data) - binary and source incompatible API break; an overloaded constructor should be added instead.
Missing @BeforeAll assumeTrue(IoUring.isAvailable()) guard (the other two new tests have it); on systems without io_uring the test fails/hangs instead of being skipped.

New condition `msgCount > 1 && in.current() instanceof ByteBuf` also disables batched scheduleWriteMultiple for datagram channels whose current message is an AddressedEnvelope (previously any msgCount > 1 batched) - broader than needed to exclude FileRegion; also redundantly re-calls in.current() instead of using msg.

If sun.nio.ch.FileChannelImpl / FileDescriptor.fd field lookup fails, JNI_OnLoad returns JNI_ERR and the whole io_uring native library fails to load on such JVMs, instead of degrading to no-FileRegion support.
Leftover commented-out `//@Disabled("Fix me")` with a pointless override that only delegates to super; new file carries a stale 'Copyright 2014' header.
SQE_FIXED_BUFFER and SQE_PERSONALITY constants are unused (layout documentation only, like the SQE_PAD_FIELD they replace).

points.point: scheduleWriteSingle returns 0 on open() failure but scheduleWrite asserts numOutstandingWrites > 0
points.note: Verified: assert at AbstractIoUringChannel.java:332. Known real bug.

points.point: ECANCELED not handled gracefully for IoUringFileRegion (ioResult throws before ECANCELED check)
points.note: Verified regression vs ByteBuf path at AbstractIoUringStreamChannel.java:375. Known real bug.

points.point: Pipe FD leak on error paths - closePipeIfNeeded only called in release()
points.note: Fabrication: handleWriteError -> close()/shutdownOutput() fail-and-release the outbound buffer (failFlushed/close -> safeRelease), which invokes IoUringFileRegion.release() -> closePipeIfNeeded(). No leak.
True but cosmetic; mirrors the previously unused SQE_PAD_FIELD they replace.

points.point: Integer truncation for large files in spliceToPipe (len and offset casts)
points.note: Verified, the known critical >2GB truncation bug; fix suggestion (drop casts, newSplice takes long off_in) is sound for offset.

points.point: validateFileRegion not called for splice-to-pipe returning 0
points.note: Fabrication: the `result == 0 && count() > 0` check in writeComplete0 runs before handleResult and is phase-agnostic - it fires for splice-to-pipe results too.

points.point: filterOutboundMessage only handles DefaultFileRegion; a comment would help
points.note: Limitation is real but already documented by the comment the PR itself adds at lines 208-210; no actionable content.

points.point: handleResult data could conflict with other uses of the data field
points.note: Speculative; stream channels have at most one outstanding write op, and data is set/consumed only by the splice path.

points.point: Test coverage gaps (partial splice, cancellation, mixed writes)

missed: Public IoUringIoOps constructor signature change is a public-API/binary break (should add overload)
IoUringFileRegionTest lacks the assumeTrue(IoUring.isAvailable()) guard the other new tests have

points.point: int transferred field overflows for files > 2GB
points.note: Verified real, part of the truncation cluster; correct fix (long transferred).

points.point: Cast truncation `(int)(count() - transferred())` for large files
points.note: Verified, the known critical >2GB truncation bug.

points.point: Unclear error for custom FileRegion implementations vs epoll/kqueue/NIO
points.note: Verified: epoll AbstractEpollStreamChannel.filterOutboundMessage:523-528 accepts any FileRegion and throws an informative EXPECTED_TYPES error; io_uring silently falls through to a bare 'unsupported message type'. Real behavioral/usability gap.

points.point: Resource leak if open() fails - IoUringFileRegion may remain unreleased with autoClose disabled; suggest fileRegion.release() before return
points.note: Fabrication: shutdownOutput() calls closeOutboundBufferForShutdown -> failFlushed/close which release the message (AbstractChannel.java:530-538); the suggested explicit release() would double-release.

points.point: Missing retry mechanism for 0-result splice-to-socket; check should verify pipeLen > 0 before validateFileRegion
points.note: Fabricated: returning false schedules POLLOUT and doWrite re-runs splice(), which dispatches on pipeLen != -1 to spliceToSocket - exactly the retry claimed missing; validateFileRegion on a non-shrunk file is a harmless no-throw. The result==0 check is phase-agnostic by design.

missed: scheduleWriteSingle returns 0 on open() failure but scheduleWrite asserts numOutstandingWrites > 0 (v7 saw the open() path but reported a fake leak instead of the real assert violation)
ECANCELED regression in the IoUringFileRegion branch of writeComplete0
Public IoUringIoOps constructor signature change is a public-API/binary break
exceeds: >2GB truncation (both the int field and the cast)
custom FileRegion error-message gap vs other transports

qualitative: v5 is the stronger review on this PR: it lands three of the four known real bugs (the critical >2GB truncation, the scheduleWriteSingle-returns-0 assert violation, and the ECANCELED regression) with accurate line references and correct fixes, but it also steps into two fabrication traps (pipe-FD leak and validateFileRegion-skipped) and misses the public-constructor API break and the missing test guard. v7 nails the truncation cluster thoroughly (both the int field and the cast) and adds a genuinely verified usability gap versus epoll's FileRegion handling, but it converts the real open()-failure bug into a fabricated leak whose suggested fix would double-release, invents a 'missing retry' that the splice() dispatch already provides, and misses both the assert violation and the ECANCELED regression outright. Both candidates exceed the single human reference point; both carry exactly two absence-fabrications, but v7's misses on the two behavioral regressions pull it below v5.
